description: Ship It (P3): config management CLI, shell completions, user API authentication, PyPI packaging with CI/CD, CLI output snapshot tests, Docker-based integration test suite, and final documentation.

userStories.title: Config management CLI
userStories.description: Implement src/kanboard_cli/commands/config_cmd.py with 5 subcommands: init, show, path, profiles, test. Replace the stub config group in main.py. Init creates ~/.config/kanboard/config.toml using tomli_w with guided prompts. Show displays current config with tokens masked. Path prints the config file path. Profiles lists all named profiles. Test calls getVersion to verify connectivity.
acceptanceCriteria: kanboard config init — creates config.toml interactively via click.prompt for URL and token; uses tomli_w to write TOML; creates directory if missing
kanboard config init does not overwrite existing config without --force flag
kanboard config show — displays the resolved config; tokens masked as ****<last4>
kanboard config path — prints the config file path (~/.config/kanboard/config.toml)
kanboard config profiles — lists all profile names from config file
kanboard config test — calls client.application.get_version() and prints connected server version
kanboard config test reports clear error when connection fails
Stub config_group in main.py replaced with real import from config_cmd module
All commands support --output table/json/csv/quiet where applicable
All public functions have docstrings
CLI tests with CliRunner covering all 5 subcommands including init file creation, show masking, and test connectivity success/failure
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: Depends on existing config system (M1 Task 4) and application resource (M3 Task 39) already on main.

userStories.title: Shell completions
userStories.description: Implement src/kanboard_cli/commands/completion.py with subcommands for generating shell completion scripts. Replace the stub completion group in main.py. Use Click's built-in shell_complete support for bash, zsh, and fish.
acceptanceCriteria: kanboard completion bash — outputs bash completion script to stdout
kanboard completion zsh — outputs zsh completion script to stdout
kanboard completion fish — outputs fish completion script to stdout
kanboard completion install <shell> — installs completion to the appropriate shell config file with instructions
Stub completion group in main.py replaced with real import
All public functions have docstrings
CLI tests with CliRunner verify each shell type outputs non-empty script content
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: User API authentication
userStories.description: Extend the config system and transport layer to support User API authentication (username + password/personal access token) in addition to the existing Application API auth. Update MeResource to make actual API calls instead of raising KanboardAuthError. Add --auth-mode CLI flag and config support.
acceptanceCriteria: Config supports auth_mode in profile: auth_mode = 'app' (default) or 'user'
Config supports username and password/token fields for user auth profiles
KANBOARD_AUTH_MODE, KANBOARD_USERNAME, KANBOARD_PASSWORD env vars supported
CLI: --auth-mode app|user global option overrides config
KanboardClient supports User API auth: HTTP Basic Auth with username + password (instead of jsonrpc + token)
MeResource methods make actual API calls when user auth is active (remove KanboardAuthError raises)
MeResource methods still raise KanboardAuthError with clear message when app auth is used
KanboardConfig.resolve() handles auth_mode, username, password resolution with layered precedence
Unit tests for config resolution with auth_mode, transport auth switching, and MeResource with both auth modes
CLI tests verify --auth-mode flag, me commands with user auth
All public methods have docstrings and full type hints
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: Unblocks integration tests for 'Me' resource. Must preserve backward compatibility for app auth (default).

userStories.title: PyPI packaging and CI/CD
userStories.description: Finalize pyproject.toml metadata for PyPI publication. Add kanboard --version support. Create GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline: lint → test → build → publish on tag.
acceptanceCriteria: pyproject.toml has complete PyPI metadata: authors, urls (homepage, repository, changelog, documentation), long_description from README
kanboard --version prints the package version (reads from importlib.metadata)
.github/workflows/ci.yml: runs on push/PR to main — lint (ruff check), test (pytest), build (hatch build)
.github/workflows/publish.yml: runs on tag push (v*) — build + publish to PyPI via trusted publisher (OIDC) or token
CI matrix tests Python 3.11 and 3.12
Build artifacts (.whl, .tar.gz) produced correctly via hatch build
All public functions have docstrings
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: Version is already in pyproject.toml. scripts/bump_version.py already handles version bumps.

userStories.title: CLI output snapshot tests
userStories.description: Create comprehensive CLI output tests via CliRunner with mocked SDK. Cover all 4 output formats (table, json, csv, quiet) systematically, plus error cases, empty results, and edge cases across all command groups.
acceptanceCriteria: Snapshot-style tests in tests/cli/test_output_snapshots.py using CliRunner with mocked KanboardClient
Tests cover at least one list and one get command per major CLI group in all 4 formats
Error output tests: KanboardNotFoundError, KanboardAPIError, KanboardAuthError displayed correctly
Empty result tests: all list commands render cleanly when returning []
Edge case tests: very long field values, None fields, special characters in output
JSON output tests verify valid JSON and correct structure (array vs object)
CSV output tests verify proper escaping of commas, quotes, newlines
Quiet output tests verify only IDs are printed, one per line
All tests follow existing CliRunner + mock patterns in tests/cli/
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: Existing CLI tests already cover per-command behavior. This story adds systematic cross-cutting format coverage.

userStories.title: Integration test infrastructure
userStories.description: Create docker-compose.test.yml with kanboard/kanboard:latest, pytest fixtures for lifecycle management, and a base integration test framework. Add make test-integration target (already exists as stub).
acceptanceCriteria: docker-compose.test.yml with kanboard/kanboard:latest service, exposed on localhost port
tests/integration/conftest.py with session-scoped fixture that waits for Kanboard to be healthy
KanboardClient fixture connected to Docker Kanboard instance
Cleanup fixture that removes test-created resources after each test/session
Helper to skip integration tests when Docker is not running (pytest.mark.integration marker)
pytest.ini or pyproject.toml registers the integration marker
Smoke integration test: connect, call getVersion, verify response
make test-integration runs only integration tests (already wired in Makefile)
All public functions have docstrings
ruff check passes, pytest passes (unit tests unaffected)
userStories.notes: Foundation for US-007 through US-009. Docker-based — tests skip gracefully when Docker unavailable.

userStories.title: Integration tests — core resources
userStories.description: CRUD lifecycle integration tests for the core resources: projects, tasks, board, columns, and swimlanes. Tests run against the Docker Kanboard instance from US-006.
acceptanceCriteria: Project lifecycle: create → get → update → enable/disable → activity → remove
Task lifecycle: create → get → update → search → open/close → move → duplicate → remove
Board: get_board returns expected column/swimlane structure for a project with tasks
Column lifecycle: add → get → update → change position → remove
Swimlane lifecycle: add → get → get_by_name → update → enable/disable → change position → remove
Tests are idempotent — clean up all created resources
Tests use descriptive names indicating the lifecycle being tested
All tests pass against kanboard/kanboard:latest Docker image
ruff check passes, pytest passes

userStories.title: Integration tests — secondary resources
userStories.description: CRUD lifecycle integration tests for secondary resources: comments, categories, tags, subtasks, users, link types, and task links. Tests run against the Docker Kanboard instance.
acceptanceCriteria: Comment lifecycle: create → get → get_all → update → remove
Category lifecycle: create → get → get_all → update → remove
Tag lifecycle: create → get_all → get_tags_by_project → set_task_tags → get_task_tags → update → remove
Subtask lifecycle: create → get → get_all → update → remove
User lifecycle: create → get → get_by_name → update → enable/disable → is_active → remove
Link type lifecycle: create → get_by_id → get_by_label → get_all → get_opposite_link_id → update → remove
Task link lifecycle: create → get → get_all → update → remove
Tests are idempotent — clean up all created resources
All tests pass against kanboard/kanboard:latest Docker image
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: US-007 provides project/task fixtures needed by these tests (comments, tags, subtasks, etc. require existing tasks/projects).

userStories.title: Integration tests — extended resources
userStories.description: CRUD lifecycle integration tests for extended resources: project files, task files, project metadata, task metadata, project permissions, groups, group members, external task links, actions, subtask time tracking, and application info. Tests run against the Docker Kanboard instance.
acceptanceCriteria: Project file lifecycle: create (upload) → get_all → get → download → remove → remove_all
Task file lifecycle: create (upload) → get_all → get → download → remove → remove_all
Project metadata lifecycle: save → get_all → get_by_name → remove
Task metadata lifecycle: save → get_all → get_by_name → remove
Project permissions: add_user → get_users → get_user_role → change_role → remove_user (and group equivalents if feasible)
Group lifecycle: create → get → get_all → update → remove
Group member lifecycle: add → get_members → get_member_groups → is_member → remove
External task link lifecycle: get_types → create → get → get_all → update → remove
Action lifecycle: get_available → get_events → get_compatible_events → create → get_actions → remove
Subtask time tracking: start → has_timer → stop → get_time_spent
Application info: get_version, get_timezone, get_colors, get_roles — all return valid data
Tests are idempotent — clean up all created resources
All tests pass against kanboard/kanboard:latest Docker image
ruff check passes, pytest passes
userStories.notes: US-007 provides project/task fixtures. Me resource tests deferred until user auth (US-003) is verified.

userStories.title: Documentation — CLI reference and SDK usage guide
userStories.description: Create docs/cli-reference.md with complete CLI command reference and docs/sdk-guide.md with SDK usage examples. Update README.md to link to these docs.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/cli-reference.md: every command group documented with usage, arguments, options, and examples
docs/cli-reference.md: global options section (--url, --token, --profile, --output, --verbose)
docs/cli-reference.md: output format examples for each format type
docs/sdk-guide.md: installation and quick start
docs/sdk-guide.md: KanboardClient initialization and context manager usage
docs/sdk-guide.md: examples for each resource category (at minimum: tasks, projects, columns, comments, tags, users)
docs/sdk-guide.md: exception handling patterns with code examples
docs/sdk-guide.md: batch API usage example
README.md updated with links to new docs
All docs use proper Markdown formatting with code blocks and section anchors
ruff check passes, pytest passes (no code changes expected)
userStories.notes: README.md already has a solid overview — these docs provide deeper reference material.

userStories.title: Documentation — configuration, workflows, and contributing
userStories.description: Create docs/configuration.md with full configuration reference, docs/workflows.md with workflow development guide, and CONTRIBUTING.md with contributor guidelines.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/configuration.md: config file format with all fields documented
docs/configuration.md: environment variable reference table
docs/configuration.md: CLI flag reference table
docs/configuration.md: resolution order explanation with examples
docs/configuration.md: named profiles setup and switching
docs/workflows.md: BaseWorkflow ABC reference with all methods/properties
docs/workflows.md: step-by-step guide to creating a workflow plugin
docs/workflows.md: workflow config section in config.toml explained
docs/workflows.md: discovery mechanism explained (directory, importlib, inspection)
CONTRIBUTING.md: development setup instructions
CONTRIBUTING.md: code style and conventions (ruff, type hints, docstrings)
CONTRIBUTING.md: testing guide (unit, CLI, integration)
CONTRIBUTING.md: PR and commit message conventions (conventional commits)
README.md updated with links to new docs and CONTRIBUTING.md
ruff check passes, pytest passes (no code changes expected)
